concept: The CSS Cascade and Specificity Resolution
code: Hello World

executionTimeline.description:
The browser parses the HTML and identifies the

element. It notes its ID, class, and inline styles.
The browser finds all CSS rules that target this element: 1. 'p' (Element), 2. '.text' (Class), 3. '#header .text' (ID + Class).

The browser assigns a weight to each rule: 
- p: (0,0,1) 
- .text: (0,1,0) 
- #header .text: (1,1,0)
The browser detects an inline style attribute 'color: red'. Inline styles have a weight of (1,0,0,0), which overrides almost everything except !important.
executionTimeline.outputTrace: Inline style detected. Overriding external CSS.

executionTimeline.description: The browser applies the winning rule. The text 'Hello World' is painted red.

theFix: To override the inline style, you would need to use the `!important` flag in your CSS (e.g., `.text { color: green !important; }`), although this is generally discouraged in professional production code in favor of cleaner architecture.
